title: campanula linifolia subsp. justiniana
intro: Campanula linifolia subsp. justiniana, also known as Justinian's bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family, characterized by its narrow, linear leaves and a tall, upright habit. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive problems, and acting as a natural antihistamine. In herbal preparations, Campanula linifolia subsp. justiniana is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being dried and used in topical applications.
